prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: One song that I find genuinely moving is "Hallelujah" by Leonard Cohen. The power of this song lies in its ability to convey deep, complex emotions through its hauntingly beautiful lyrics and melody. The juxtaposition of themes—love, loss, faith, and doubt—creates a raw and authentic expression of the human experience. Each verse feels like a whispered confession, drawing the listener into a vulnerable, intimate space. The fluctuating dynamics and emotionally charged delivery amplify the sense of turmoil and resolution, making it a profoundly resonant piece of art. Whether sung by Cohen himself or covered by countless artists, "Hallelujah" never fails to evoke a visceral emotional response.
